mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits easier discounting and supports equity multiples, so lower policy rates predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive more often than negative after FEDFUNDS declines for 2 consecutive months, or if the sign of the rate-change signal is inverted in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates above the prior 3-month trend, real policy tightens, discount rates rise, and equity valuations compress, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-acceleration triggers are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the interaction with FEDFUNDS does not improve out-of-sample classification versus CPIAUCSL alone.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals weakening household income and demand, which leads equity investors to price lower near-term corporate revenues and negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-rise triggers do not precede negative 20-day SPX returns at a rate above 50%, or if the signal loses directional accuracy in late-cycle periods for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth in PAYEMS above trend confirms labor-market strength and supports consumption and earnings expectations, so accelerating employment growth predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises faster than its 6-month average by 0.2% or more
failure_condition: Reject if PAYEMS acceleration fails to align with positive 20-day SPX returns in at least 55% of triggered months, or if adding RSAFS does not materially outperform PAYEMS alone.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in RSAFS implies stronger current consumer spending and revenue realization, which should lift near-term earnings expectations and generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Reject if high-RSAFS-growth months are not followed by positive 5-day SPX returns more often than negative ones, or if the effect disappears after controlling for the prior month trend.

mechanism_narrative: Weak INDPRO and soft HOUST together indicate broad production slowdown and fading cyclical momentum, which should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous declines in INDPRO and HOUST do not precede negative 60-day SPX returns in at least 60% of cases, or if either series alone explains the signal equally well.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in HOUST after a prior trough signals housing-cycle stabilization, improved wealth effects, and better cyclically sensitive earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises for 2 consecutive months after being below its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if housing stabilization triggers are not followed by positive 60-day SPX returns more often than negative ones, or if the effect is absent when housing starts rise from depressed levels.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, a FEDFUNDS rate above 2% combined with accelerating CPIAUCSL implies real-rate pressure and multiple compression, so equities should underperform over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 2.0% and CPIAUCSL 6-month inflation trend is rising
failure_condition: Reject if the joint tightening-and-inflation condition does not produce negative 60-day SPX returns in at least 55% of triggered instances, or if the signal is not stronger than either proxy used independently.
